statements.sql: SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName INTO silver.SelectIntoTarget FROM bronze.Product

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName FROM bronze.Product
refactor.refactored_sql: with import as ( select ProductID, ProductName from bronze.Product ), logical as ( select cast(ProductID as nvarchar(25)) as ProductAlternateKey, ProductName as EnglishProductName from import ), final as ( select * from logical ) select * from final

output_columns.summary: Both queries return ProductAlternateKey (NVARCHAR(25)) and EnglishProductName (NVARCHAR(50)). Refactored query wraps extraction in CTEs but maintains identical column names and types.

joins.summary: No joins in either query; both are single-table selects

filters.summary: Neither query applies filters; both are unfiltered selects from the source

aggregation_grain.summary: Both queries operate at row level with no aggregation functions; grain is equivalent
